#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu

echo "==== Installing Visual Studio Code (.deb) ===="

TMP_DIR="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$TMP_DIR"' EXIT
cd "$TMP_DIR"

fetch() {
  URL="$1"
  OUT="$2"
  if command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then  
    curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
  elif command -v wget >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    wget -q "$URL" -O "$OUT"
  else
    if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "curl/wget not found; installing curl..."
      apt-get update
      apt-get install -y curl
      curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
    else
      echo "Error: neither curl nor wget is available, and no apt-get to install them."
      exit 127
    fi
  fi
}

echo "Downloading VS Code .deb package..."
fetch "https://code.visualstudio.com/sha/download?build=stable&os=linux-deb-x64" "vscode.deb"

if ! command -v dpkg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Error: dpkg not found. This script supports Debian/Ubuntu images only."
  exit 1
fi

echo "Installing VS Code..."
# Try install; if deps missing, fix them and retry
if ! dpkg -i vscode.deb 2>/dev/null; then
  if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    apt-get update
    apt-get install -y -f
    dpkg -i vscode.deb
  else
    echo "Error: apt-get not available to resolve dependencies."
    exit 1
  fi
fi

# Simple, build-safe verification (no GUI/sandbox)
if command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1 && dpkg -s code >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "VS Code installed successfully:"
  dpkg -s code | awk -F': ' '/^Version:/{print "Version:", $2}'
else
  echo "Error: VS Code installation failed."
  exit 1
fi

# Ensure the folder exists
mkdir -p /root/capsule/code/vscode-data

# Upgrade pip and install Jupyter
echo "Installing/Upgrading pip and Jupyter..."
python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip
python3 -m pip install jupyter

# Install VS Code extensions for Python + Jupyter
# (These will go into /root/.vscode-server/extensions or ~/.vscode/extensions)
if command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Installing VS Code Python and Jupyter extensions..."
  code --install-extension ms-python.python --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox
  code --install-extension ms-toolsai.jupyter --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox
else
  echo "Warning: 'code' command not found — skipping VS Code extension installation."
fi

# Create a system-wide alias for all future shells
# now you can simply run 'code' in Terminal
echo "alias code='code --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox'" >> /etc/bash.bashrc

# installing utilities and cleaning apt caches
apt-get update
apt-get install -y numlockx gthumb
rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
apt-get clean
rm -rf /var/cache/apt/archives/*

# ensure pip tooling is up-to-date
python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel

python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
  pandas \
  seaborn \
  matplotlib \
  scipy \
  tifffile \
  zarr \
  "numpy==1.26.4" \
  "opencv-python-headless==4.11.0.86" \
  codeocean==0.14.0 \
  aind_session \
  pytest \
  pillow \
  pandas \
  pyyaml \
  aind-ophys-utils \
  pymongo \
  ipykernel \
  scikit-learn \
  suite2p \
  pyqtgraph \
  pyqt5 \
  'napari[all]' \
  'pydantic<2.11' \
  plotly \
  anywidget \
  ipympl

echo "Registering Python kernel for Jupyter..."
python3 -m ipykernel install --sys-prefix --name python3 --display-name "Python 3"
python3 -m jupyter kernelspec list

cd /
git clone https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/comb
cd comb
pip install -e .
#git checkout 21b9cae9431f7f83e1973af6f1fc1ecd40fc765d
git checkout for_gcamp_validation

cd /
git clone https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/lamf-analysis
cd lamf-analysis
pip install -e .

# Update Fiji
FIJI_DIR=/Fiji.app
FIJI="$FIJI_DIR/ImageJ-linux64"
# Update all components that the Updater would select by default
"$FIJI" --update update

echo "Fiji/BDV ecosystem updated."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu

echo "==== Installing Visual Studio Code (.deb) ===="

TMP_DIR="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$TMP_DIR"' EXIT
cd "$TMP_DIR"

fetch() {
  URL="$1"
  OUT="$2"
  if command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then  
    curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
  elif command -v wget >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    wget -q "$URL" -O "$OUT"
  else
    if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "curl/wget not found; installing curl..."
      apt-get update
      apt-get install -y curl
      curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
    else
      echo "Error: neither curl nor wget is available, and no apt-get to install them."
      exit 127
    fi
  fi
}

echo "Downloading VS Code .deb package..."
fetch "https://code.visualstudio.com/sha/download?build=stable&os=linux-deb-x64" "vscode.deb"

if ! command -v dpkg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Error: dpkg not found. This script supports Debian/Ubuntu images only."
  exit 1
fi

echo "Installing VS Code..."
# Try install; if deps missing, fix them and retry
if ! dpkg -i vscode.deb 2>/dev/null; then
  if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    apt-get update
    apt-get install -y -f
    dpkg -i vscode.deb
  else
    echo "Error: apt-get not available to resolve dependencies."
    exit 1
  fi
fi

# Simple, build-safe verification (no GUI/sandbox)
if command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1 && dpkg -s code >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "VS Code installed successfully:"
  dpkg -s code | awk -F': ' '/^Version:/{print "Version:", $2}'
else
  echo "Error: VS Code installation failed."
  exit 1
fi

# Ensure the folder exists
mkdir -p /root/capsule/code/vscode-data

# Upgrade pip and install Jupyter
echo "Installing/Upgrading pip and Jupyter..."
python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip
python3 -m pip install jupyter

# Install VS Code extensions for Python + Jupyter
# (These will go into /root/.vscode-server/extensions or ~/.vscode/extensions)
if command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Installing VS Code Python and Jupyter extensions..."
  code --install-extension ms-python.python --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox
  code --install-extension ms-toolsai.jupyter --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox
else
  echo "Warning: 'code' command not found — skipping VS Code extension installation."
fi

# Create a system-wide alias for all future shells
# now you can simply run 'code' in Terminal
echo "alias code='code --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox'" >> /etc/bash.bashrc

# installing utilities and cleaning apt caches
apt-get update
apt-get install -y numlockx gthumb
rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
apt-get clean
rm -rf /var/cache/apt/archives/*

# ensure pip tooling is up-to-date
python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel

python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
  pandas \
  seaborn \

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu

echo "==== Installing Visual Studio Code (.deb) ===="

TMP_DIR="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$TMP_DIR"' EXIT
cd "$TMP_DIR"

fetch() {
  URL="$1"
  OUT="$2"
  if command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then  
    curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
  elif command -v wget >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    wget -q "$URL" -O "$OUT"
  else
    if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "curl/wget not found; installing curl..."
      apt-get update
      apt-get install -y curl
      curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$OUT"
    else
      echo "Error: neither curl nor wget is available, and no apt-get to install them."
      exit 127
    fi
  fi
}

echo "Downloading VS Code .deb package..."
fetch "https://code.visualstudio.com/sha/download?build=stable&os=linux-deb-x64" "vscode.deb"

if ! command -v dpkg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Error: dpkg not found. This script supports Debian/Ubuntu images only."
  exit 1
fi

echo "Installing VS Code..."
# Try install; if deps missing, fix them and retry
if ! dpkg -i vscode.deb 2>/dev/null; then
  if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    apt-get update
    apt-get install -y -f
    dpkg -i vscode.deb
  else
    echo "Error: apt-get not available to resolve dependencies."
    exit 1
  fi
fi

# Simple, build-safe verification (no GUI/sandbox)
if command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1 && dpkg -s code >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "VS Code installed successfully:"
  dpkg -s code | awk -F': ' '/^Version:/{print "Version:", $2}'
else
  echo "Error: VS Code installation failed."
  exit 1
fi

# Ensure the folder exists
mkdir -p /root/capsule/code/vscode-data

# Upgrade pip and install Jupyter
echo "Installing/Upgrading pip and Jupyter..."
python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip
python3 -m pip install jupyter

# Install VS Code extensions for Python + Jupyter
# (These will go into /root/.vscode-server/extensions or ~/.vscode/extensions)
if command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Installing VS Code Python and Jupyter extensions..."
  code --install-extension ms-python.python --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox
  code --install-extension ms-toolsai.jupyter --force --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox
else
  echo "Warning: 'code' command not found — skipping VS Code extension installation."
fi

# Create a system-wide alias for all future shells
# now you can simply run 'code' in Terminal
echo "alias code='code --user-data-dir=/root/capsule/code/vscode-data --no-sandbox'" >> /etc/bash.bashrc

# installing utilities and cleaning apt caches
apt-get update
apt-get install -y numlockx gthumb
rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
apt-get clean
rm -rf /var/cache/apt/archives/*

# ensure pip tooling is up-to-date
python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel

python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
  pandas \
  seaborn \
  matplotlib \
  scipy \
  tifffile \
  zarr \
  "numpy==1.26.4" \
  "opencv-python-headless==4.11.0.86" \
  codeocean==0.14.0 \
  aind_session \
  pytest \
  pillow \
  pandas \
  pyyaml \
  aind-ophys-utils \
  pymongo \
  ipykernel \
  scikit-learn \
  suite2p \
  pyqtgraph \
  pyqt5 \
  'napari[all]' \
  'pydantic<2.11' \
  plotly \
  anywidget \
  ipympl

echo "Registering Python kernel for Jupyter..."
python3 -m ipykernel install --sys-prefix --name python3 --display-name "Python 3"
python3 -m jupyter kernelspec list

echo "Configuring workspace VS Code settings for stable Python/Jupyter detection..."
mkdir -p /root/capsule/.vscode
cat > /root/capsule/.vscode/settings.json << 'EOF'
{
  "python-envs.defaultEnvManager": "ms-python.python:venv",
  "python-envs.defaultPackageManager": "ms-python.python:pip",
  "python.defaultInterpreterPath": "/opt/conda/bin/python",
  "python.condaPath": "/opt/conda/bin/conda",
  "jupyter.jupyterServerType": "local",
  "jupyter.kernels.excludePythonEnvironments": [],
  "python.terminal.activateEnvironment": true,
  "files.watcherExclude": {
    "**/.git/**": true,
    "**/data/**": true,
    "**/results/**": true,
    "**/scratch/**": true
  },
  "search.exclude": {
    "**/data/**": true,
    "**/results/**": true,
    "**/scratch/**": true
  }
}
EOF

cd /
git clone https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/comb
cd comb
pip install -e .

git checkout for_gcamp_validation

cd /
git clone https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/lamf-analysis
cd lamf-analysis
pip install -e .

# Update Fiji
FIJI_DIR=/Fiji.app
FIJI="$FIJI_DIR/ImageJ-linux64"
# Update all components that the Updater would select by default
"$FIJI" --update update

echo "Fiji/BDV ecosystem updated."

{
    "python-envs.defaultEnvManager": "ms-python.python:venv",
    "python-envs.defaultPackageManager": "ms-python.python:pip",
    "python-envs.workspaceSearchPaths": [],
